preamble: Backing of [ε] to a mid-back vowel [o] or [ɔ] typically occurs under the influence of neighboring sounds, such as dark 'l' or historical breaking processes.

examples.note: Pre-L coalescence/backing: [ɛ] backs toward [o/ɔ] before dark L [ɫ] in Cockney and L-vocalizing dialects.
OE Breaking: short e → diphthong eo before r/l + consonant. Strictly ε→eo, not ε→o; the [o] component is the second element of the diphthong.
